source_pr.title: plans:fix a bug in index plan
source_pr.body:
When we have a interger column type, and compare it with a float value, the previous implementation first convert that float value to the column's type interger, then the value may be changed.

for example, we have column c1 values `1, 3, 5, 7`, when condition is `c1 >= 3.1` the result should be `5, 7`, but as we first converted 3.1 to 3, the condition has changed to `c1 >= 3`, then we get the wrong result `3, 5, 7`.

But type convertion is nessesary because the seek value must have the same type as the column to be encoded corectly.

To fix this bug, I added a field in `indexSpan`, which is the type converted value used to seek the lower bound, and keep the original `lowVal` for comparison.

I also added a `testkit` package which simplifies testing code.

symptoms: "select * from t1, t2 order by t1.c1, t1.c2, t2.c1, t2.c2", (plan/plans/join_test.go)
failure_signature: "select * from t1, t2 order by t1.c1, t1.c2, t2.c1, t2.c2", (plan/plans/join_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: "select * from t1, t2 order by t1.c1, t1.c2, t2.c1, t2.c2", (plan/plans/join_test.go)

files: expression/binop.go, expression/binop_test.go, expression/visitor_ident.go, expression/visitor_test.go, field/result_field.go, plan/plans/from.go, plan/plans/index.go, plan/plans/index_test.go, plan/plans/join.go, plan/plans/join_test.go, util/testkit/testkit.go
